answer:
The following works in the Falsafa corpus mention mountains, hills, or peaks (primarily in translation). I've grouped them by unique work, noting relevant chapters and contexts with example citations.[^1][^2][^3]

**Charles Comte, *Traité de la propriété*: VOL I**  
Discusses deforestation of mountains and its effects on rivers, including rainwater on mountain crests and hills.[^4]

**Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1***  
Poetic references to lunar hills, Himalayan peaks, and peaks as Sinai.[^5]

**Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie*: VOL I**  
Mentions small hills in the context of climate and geography in Mexico.[^6]

**Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie*: VOL II**  
References high hills, valleys, and crossing mountains via canals/aqueducts.[^7]

**Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation*: VOL IV**  
Compares slave labor to free peasants climbing steep hillsides.[^8]

**Cynewulf, *Elene***  
References Stephen being stoned on a hill.[^9]

**Unknown, *Manusmṛti***  
Mentions types of forts, including hill-forts.[^10]

**Unknown, *Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana***  
Describes peaks of Mount Semeru filled with sages.[^11]

**Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2***  
Poetic references to verdant hills and Sinai's hill.[^12]

**Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib***  
Mentions a litter on a hill and peaks/crests metaphorically.[^13]

**Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation*: VOL III**  
Describes mountains of rubble (from collapsed buildings) around Egyptian cities.[^14]

[^1]: Results drawn from full-corpus search on "mountain OR mountains OR hill OR hills OR peak OR peaks"; 19 chapter matches across 11 works (no exhaustive chapter reads performed).
[^2]: *Traité de la propriété*: VOL I, Ch. 13, [paragraph](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-i-2c7a99/13-influence-du-deboisement-des-montagnes-sur-les-fle/translation/#p-266536).
[^3]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, Ch. 19, [paragraph](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/19-ehd-e-tifli/translation/#p-09d3e2).
[^4]: *Traité de la propriété*: VOL I, Ch. 13, [paragraph](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-i-2c7a99/13-influence-du-deboisement-des-montagnes-sur-les-fle/translation/#p-266536).
[^5]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, Ch. 19, [paragraph](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/19-ehd-e-tifli/translation/#p-09d3e2).
[^6]: *Nouveau traité d'économie*: VOL I, Ch. 3, [paragraph](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/03-ii-influence-des-circonstances-exterieures-sur-la-/translation/#p-5c2016).
[^7]: *Nouveau traité d'économie*: VOL II, Ch. 15, [paragraph](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/15-v-application-de-ces-moyens-de-liberte-aux-diverse/translation/#p-8f4bb3).
[^8]: *Traité de Législation*: VOL IV, Ch. 13, [paragraph](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iv-bca040/13-de-l-influence-de-l-esclavage-domestique-sur-la-pr/translation/#p-eb7578).
[^9]: *Elene*, Ch. 1, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-elene-d2d132/01/translation/#p-f5d1f1).
[^10]: *Manusmṛti*, Ch. 7, [paragraph](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/07/translation/#p-db91b7).
[^11]: *Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana*, Ch. 1, [paragraph](/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-a401fd).
[^12]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, Ch. 10, [paragraph](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/10-ek-shaam/translation/#p-7a445e).
[^13]: *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, Ch. 186, [paragraph](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/186-shumar-e-subha-marghoob-e-but-e-mushkil-pasand-aay/translation/#p-f8f63c).
[^14]: *Traité de Législation*: VOL III, Ch. 33, [paragraph](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iii-ca589a/33-des-relations-observees-entre-l-aristocratie-milit/translation/#p-b26884).
